meta_title: For Students: Claude Code skills setup to Solve stuck with basic apps (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to Claude Code skills setup and overcome stuck with basic apps. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: stuck with basic apps

content.seo_title: Claude Code Skills Setup: Escape the Basic App Trap (2025)
content.seo_description: Stop building basic CRUD apps. Master Claude Code and Antigravity to architect production-grade AI software. A high-impact checklist for ambitious students.
content.answer_block: Break through the 'tutorial hell' barrier by integrating Claude Code into your local environment. By leveraging Antigravity for architectural scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative implementation, you bridge the gap between simple scripts and sophisticated, scalable AI applications, effectively turning your student projects into professional-grade engineering portfolio pieces.
content.intro:
You have built the to-do lists and the simple weather apps. You know the syntax, but you feel the ceiling—that frustrating wall where your code becomes a tangled mess of spaghetti the moment you try to add real AI intelligence. You are trapped in the 'beginner loop,' watching your potential stall while the industry moves toward agentic workflows.

This isn't about writing more code; it is about writing better code faster. By mastering the synergy between Antigravity’s structural foresight and Claude Code’s iterative execution, you will stop struggling with boilerplate and start architecting complex, autonomous systems. It is time to stop being a student who codes and start being an engineer who builds.

steps.title: Environment Hardening
steps.description: Configure your CLI environment to handle high-context AI interactions without latency or permission bottlenecks.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current project structure and suggest a modular architecture that supports future integration of LLM-based agents.

steps.title: Architectural Scaffolding
steps.description: Use Antigravity to define your system's core abstractions before writing a single line of business logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a robust directory structure for a production-ready AI app, including folders for services, agents, and utilities.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --plan 'Initialize project structure based on Antigravity spec'

steps.title: Iterative Feature Implementation
steps.description: Break complex features into atomic tasks to ensure Claude Code maintains high output quality and strict type safety.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Decompose the user authentication module into three distinct, testable development phases.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Implement OAuth2 integration with secure session handling'

steps.title: Automated Testing Loops
steps.description: Integrate automated feedback loops to ensure your AI-generated code meets production standards.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate unit tests for the newly created service layer, focusing on edge cases and error handling.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --test 'Verify all service layer functions against Antigravity test suite'

steps.title: Refactoring for Scalability
steps.description: Pass your codebase through an architectural audit to optimize performance and reduce technical debt.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Audit the codebase for potential performance bottlenecks and suggest refactoring patterns for better modularity.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --refactor 'Optimize database queries and clean up redundant imports'

pro_tips: Always force Claude Code to generate a README.md at the end of every major sprint to maintain project documentation automatically.
Use Antigravity to generate 'negative test cases'—scenarios where your AI agent should purposefully fail to ensure graceful degradation.
Keep your context window clean by periodically clearing Claude Code’s history when switching between distinct architectural modules.
Define a 'System Prompt' file in your root folder and instruct Claude Code to reference it during every session for consistent coding style.
Use local Git branching for every Claude Code task; if an AI-generated implementation misses the mark, you can revert to a clean state in milliseconds.
content.next_steps: Deploy your project to a cloud provider like Vercel or Railway and document your architectural decisions in a public GitHub repository to attract recruiters.
content.conversion_block: Stop dreaming about building impactful software and start shipping it. You now have the toolkit to bridge the gap between student projects and production-grade applications. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
